questions.q: Lachit Borphukan was a military commander serving which kingdom?
questions.explanation: Lachit Borphukan was a general of the Ahom Kingdom of the Brahmaputra valley in Assam.

questions.q: Lachit Borphukan led the defence of Assam against which great power?
questions.explanation: He led the Ahom defence against repeated Mughal invasions in the 17th century CE.

questions.q: For which decisive 1671 battle is Lachit Borphukan most celebrated?
questions.explanation: He is famed for the decisive victory at the Battle of Saraighat in 1671.

questions.q: The Battle of Saraighat was fought primarily on which kind of terrain?
questions.explanation: Saraighat was largely a naval battle fought on the Brahmaputra river.

questions.q: In which century did Lachit Borphukan live and fight?
questions.explanation: Lachit Borphukan lived in the 17th century CE, with Saraighat fought in 1671.

questions.q: The Ahom Kingdom that Lachit defended was centred on the valley of which river?
questions.explanation: The Ahom Kingdom ruled the Brahmaputra valley of present-day Assam.

questions.q: In which present-day Indian state did Lachit Borphukan win his famous victory?
questions.explanation: Saraighat lies near present-day Guwahati in Assam.

questions.q: What did the Ahom victory at Saraighat achieve?
options: It halted the Mughal advance into Assam
questions.explanation: The victory halted the Mughal advance into the region and preserved Ahom independence.

questions.q: What does the title 'Borphukan' that Lachit held signify?
options: A high Ahom military and administrative commander
questions.explanation: Borphukan was a senior Ahom office combining military command with regional governance.

questions.q: Lachit Borphukan is remembered today chiefly as a symbol of what?
questions.explanation: He is celebrated as a symbol of Assamese resistance and patriotism.

questions.q: Which Ahom king did Lachit Borphukan serve as commander during the Saraighat campaign?
questions.explanation: Lachit served the Ahom king Chakradhwaj Singha, who resolved to drive the Mughals out.

questions.q: Which Mughal commander led the imperial forces defeated at Saraighat?
questions.explanation: The Mughal army at Saraighat was led by Raja Ram Singh I of Amber.

questions.q: Lachit famously beheaded his own maternal uncle for negligence during the building of which defensive structure?
questions.explanation: He executed his uncle for shoddy work on a defensive rampart, declaring his uncle was not greater than his country.

questions.q: Which famous saying is attributed to Lachit during the rampart-building episode?
options: "My uncle is not greater than my country."
questions.explanation: He is said to have declared that his uncle was not greater than his country before executing him.

questions.q: What advantage did Lachit deliberately exploit by drawing the Mughals into a fight on the Brahmaputra?
options: The Mughals' weakness in riverine and naval warfare
questions.explanation: Ahom strength lay in riverine warfare, where Mughal cavalry and conventional tactics were neutralised.

questions.q: Saraighat was fought near which modern Assamese city?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Saraighat was fought on the Brahmaputra near present-day Guwahati.

questions.q: According to tradition, in what physical condition did Lachit lead his men in the final phase of Saraighat?
options: He was gravely ill yet still commanded from his boat
He was a captive directing from afar
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that a gravely ill Lachit rallied his wavering forces from his war-boat, turning the tide.

questions.q: Roughly how long after the Battle of Saraighat did Lachit Borphukan die?
questions.explanation: Lachit died around 1672, soon after Saraighat, reportedly from the illness he had suffered during the battle.

questions.q: The Ahoms originally migrated into Assam from which broader region?
questions.explanation: The Ahoms descended from Tai migrants who entered Assam from Southeast Asia in the 13th century.

questions.q: Before Saraighat, the Mughals had earlier captured which Ahom area, prompting the campaign Lachit reversed?
questions.explanation: Lachit recaptured Guwahati from the Mughals before the climactic stand at Saraighat.

questions.q: Which annual award, named after Lachit, is given to the best cadet at a prominent Indian military academy?
questions.explanation: The best passing-out cadet at the National Defence Academy receives the Lachit Borphukan Gold Medal.

questions.q: Lachit's father, who also held high Ahom office, was named what?
questions.explanation: Lachit was the son of Momai Tamuli Borbarua, a senior Ahom official.

questions.q: The earlier Mughal general who had invaded Assam and forced a humiliating treaty before Lachit's campaigns was who?
questions.explanation: Mir Jumla's invasion of 1662 had earlier penetrated deep into Assam, setting the stage for the Ahom resurgence under Lachit.

questions.q: The 1671 victory came after the Mughals were lured into open river battle following the failure of which tactic?
options: A cavalry charge across the Ganges
A negotiated single combat / a stalled land assault near the hills
questions.explanation: After land manoeuvres and stalled approaches near the hills, the Mughals were drawn onto the river where Ahom naval power prevailed.

questions.q: What is the meaning of the place name 'Saraighat' associated with the battle site?
options: A ghat (riverbank landing) on the Brahmaputra near Guwahati
A trading port on the coast
questions.explanation: Saraighat was a ghat or riverbank landing on the Brahmaputra near Guwahati, giving the battle its name.

questions.q: The Ahom court chronicles that record Lachit's deeds are collectively known as what?
questions.explanation: The Buranjis are the historical chronicles of the Ahom court that preserve accounts of Lachit and Saraighat.

questions.q: Lachit's small, swift Ahom war-boats were key to Saraighat. These vessels suited the Brahmaputra because they were what?
options: Light, manoeuvrable, and able to swarm larger ships
questions.explanation: Light and manoeuvrable Ahom boats could swarm and harry the larger, less agile Mughal flotilla on the river.

questions.q: Lachit Borphukan Divas (Lachit Day) is observed each year in Assam on which date?
questions.explanation: Assam observes Lachit Divas on 24 November to commemorate his heroism.

questions.q: Which large statue of Lachit, unveiled in recent years, stands on an island in the Brahmaputra at Guwahati?
options: The statue on Brahmaputra's mid-river island near Guwahati
questions.explanation: A prominent statue of Lachit Borphukan stands on a mid-river island in the Brahmaputra at Guwahati.

questions.q: The Ahom Kingdom that Lachit served endured, despite later setbacks, until it was finally absorbed by whom in the early 19th century?
options: The British, after the Treaty of Yandabo (1826)
questions.explanation: The Ahom Kingdom eventually passed to the British following the Treaty of Yandabo in 1826, long after Lachit's death.
